name: Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop
name_original: Aguarales de Valdemilaz — running desde Valpalmas
description: Jog this 6.3-mile (10.1 km) moderate route from Valpalmas to explore the unique "fairy chimney" formations of Aguarales de Valdemilaz.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Cinco Villas traverse a diverse landscape, transitioning from pre-Pyrenean mountains in the north to expansive plains in the south. The region features varied terrain, including deep valleys, dense pine forests, and unique geological formations like the Aguarales de Valpalmas. Numerous lagoons and reservoirs, such as Moncayuelo Lagoon and Valdelafuén, also offer scenic paths for running. Best jogging routes around Cinco Villas The most popular jogging route is Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique clay formations of the Aguarales. Another top favourite among local runners is Luesia - Puy Moné Loop, a difficult 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, reaching the Puy Moné peak. Local runners also love the Sanctuary of Monlora Loop, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Santo Domingo, often completed in about 52 minutes. Jogging around Cinco Villas is defined by pre-Pyrenean mountains, vast plains, and numerous water bodies, including lagoons and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Cinco Villas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Cinco Villas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cinco Villas?
A: Cinco Villas offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 trails available for exploration. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from pre-Pyrenean mountains to expansive plains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Cinco Villas?
A: The region boasts remarkably diverse terrain, transitioning from pre-Pyrenean mountains in the north, like the Sierra de Santo Domingo, to vast plains further south. You'll find deep valleys, dense pine forests, arid zones such as The Bardenas Negras, and unique geological formations like the Aguarales de Valpalmas. This variety ensures a dynamic visual and physical experience for runners.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Cinco Villas?
A: Yes, Cinco Villas offers routes suitable for various fitness levels. While many trails are moderate to difficult, there are at least 2 easy routes available, providing gentler options for those new to running or looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the running routes in Cinco Villas?
A: Many running routes in Cinco Villas pass by stunning natural attractions. You can explore unique clay formations at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;, discover refreshing natural pools like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2716013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pígalo Pools&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Fonguera Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features numerous lagoons and reservoirs, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5229495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanca de El Bolaso&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Bartolomé Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, many of which have ornithological routes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cinco Villas?
A: Yes, many routes in Cinco Villas are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983266031/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983181671/luesia-puy-mone-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia - Puy Moné Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983632191/sanctuary-of-monlora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Cinco Villas?
A: The running routes in Cinco Villas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from numerous reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous challenges to serene lakeside paths, and the opportunity to explore unique natural features and historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Cinco Villas offers several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983181671/luesia-puy-mone-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia - Puy Moné Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.4-mile path with substantial ascent, leading to the Puy Moné peak. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261718/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-running-from-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — running from Luna&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 18 kilometers.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, Cinco Villas is home to several viewpoints that offer spectacular panoramic vistas, especially in the pre-Pyrenean areas. Routes through the Sierra de Santo Domingo, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983632191/sanctuary-of-monlora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora Loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide elevated perspectives. Strategic viewpoints near the Sanctuary of Monllor, the castle of Sora, and the chapel of Sancho Abarca are excellent spots to take in the scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Cinco Villas?
A: Cinco Villas offers enjoyable running conditions throughout much of the year due to its varied climate. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures, ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially in the plains, but higher elevation routes in the Sierra de Santo Domingo offer cooler conditions. Winter running is also possible, particularly on lower-lying trails.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water bodies or wetlands?
A: Yes, Cinco Villas features numerous lagoons and reservoirs, providing picturesque running paths. The region includes Moncayuelo Lagoon, and reservoirs like Gancho, Moncayuelo, Bolaso, Valdelafuén, San Bartolomé, and Erla. Many of these areas, such as the natural site of Valdelafuén, are classified as special bird protection areas and offer peaceful, visually appealing routes amidst abundant birdlife. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanca del Gancho – La Llana Viewpoint loop from Ejea de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience this.

Q: Are there any routes that explore unique geological formations?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its distinctive geological features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983266031/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you through the whimsical, tubular clay formations of the Aguarales de Valpalmas, offering a truly unique running experience.
tips:
Valpalmas is a small town in the province of Zaragoza, Aragon, known for its stunning natural landscape.
The town has a rich history and traditions that you can discover by exploring its streets. The town's architecture and atmosphere reflect its cultural heritage.

The Aguarales de Valpalmas represent the modeling power of water on rocks. A place that will arouse our curiosity and that of the little ones due to the spectacular nature of its forms.
We will find points to observe these geological formations and signs with more information apart from being able to walk among them.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The jogging route starts in Valpalmas. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as the base for exploring the Aguarales de Valdemilaz. The trailhead is easily accessible from the village.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is considered to have an intermediate physical difficulty for jogging due to its moderate length and elevation changes. While not extremely technical, the mixed and uneven terrain requires a moderate level of fitness and awareness, so it might be a bit challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of surfaces, including sections of unpaved road and track, as well as narrower trails. You'll jog through steppe landscapes, Mediterranean forests, and scrubland. Near the Aguarales de Valdemilaz, the ground is characterized by fragile clay, sand, and gypsum, creating uneven 'badlands' terrain with pinnacles, ravines, and gullies. Good foot placement is essential.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route is suitable for jogging any time of year except during the summer months due to potentially high temperatures. Spring and autumn offer more pleasant conditions for exploring the unique landscape.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the fragile geological formations and potential for wildlife. Ensure your dog is comfortable with varied terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Aguarales de Valdemilaz or to jog this route from Valpalmas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Aguarales de Valdemilaz themselves, often called 'fairy chimneys' or a 'mini Cappadocia,' which are unique geological formations. You'll also pass by the Valpalmas Church at the start/end of your run. The route offers various viewpoints to appreciate the surreal, almost lunar landscape.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This specific route, 'Aguarales de Valdemilaz — running from Valpalmas,' is an out-and-back trail. You will return along the same path to your starting point in Valpalmas.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for sun exposure, it's essential to bring plenty of water, especially outside of cooler months, as provisions are only available in Valpalmas. Wearing a cap or hat is advisable. Sturdy jogging shoes with good grip are recommended for the uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Valpalmas can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from larger nearby towns in Zaragoza province, as direct routes might not be frequent. Driving is often the most convenient way to reach Valpalmas.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trailhead in Valpalmas?
faqs.answer: Valpalmas is a small village, but it does offer some basic amenities. You might find a local bar or small shop for refreshments. For more extensive options like restaurants or accommodation, you may need to look in larger nearby towns.
